authoritative_sources.title: 2022 NAICS Manual, Sector 56 and Subsector 561 Administrative and Support Services
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Census Bureau and Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official U.S. industry taxonomy used to define the administrative-support and business-services operating boundary and prevent generic analytics treatment.

authoritative_sources.title: Administrative and Support Services: NAICS 561
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official public industry profile confirming administrative and support service operating contexts used to shape service-family, queue, workforce, and support-service assumptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Industry terminology and operating context validation

authoritative_sources.title: OMB Circular No. A-11, Section 280 - Managing Customer Experience and Improving Service Delivery
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative public guidance for service delivery, priority services, post-transaction feedback datasets, quarterly reporting, benchmarking, touchpoints, and service improvement actions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Metric cadence, service touchpoint, customer feedback, and reporting design

authoritative_sources.title: Commission Guidance on Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative public metric-disclosure guidance for KPI definition, calculation, usefulness, management use, assumptions, methodology changes, and disclosure controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Metric definition, methodology-change disclosure, and unsupported-claim refusal

authoritative_sources.title: Universal Electronic Records Management Requirements
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. National Archives and Records Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official records-management requirements source for capture, metadata, retention, disposition, access, and auditability expectations in administrative service records systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Records capture, evidence retention, disposition, and traceability

authoritative_sources.title: Unauthorized Dispositions
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. National Archives and Records Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official NARA failure-handling guidance for records that are unlawfully removed, altered, deleted, destroyed, or corrupted, which constrains analytics based on missing or corrupted evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government, GAO-25-107721
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative public internal-control framework for operations, reporting, compliance, information quality, control activities, monitoring, deficiencies, and remediation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Control mapping, information quality, monitoring, exception escalation, and remediation

authoritative_sources.title: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations, SP 800-53 Rev. 5
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Authoritative public control catalog for access control, audit/accountability, privacy, system integrity, and evidence controls used by administrative service systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Access control, audit logging, privacy caveats, and system evidence checks

workflow_stages.name: Intake and service analytics classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture request intent, client/SOW scope, service family, service catalog item, metric purpose, audience, cadence, owner matrix, publication context, and system-of-record context before analysis.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Metric definition and source hierarchy
workflow_stages.description: Map each metric to service records, denominator/exclusion rules, refresh windows, source precedence, calculation method, assumptions, and methodology-change requirements.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Source collection and evidence validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect and validate service desk records, workflow queue states, document repository indexes, approval packets, audit logs, CRM context, BI extracts, retention/access metadata, and exception registers.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Report assembly, caveats, and confidence scoring
workflow_stages.description: Prepare metric packages, trend notes, reconciliation summaries, and action-threshold briefs with audience-specific caveats and confidence posture.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Disclosure, conflict, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate stale, incomplete, conflicting, restricted, or unsupported data; refuse unsupported causal, benchmark, policy, forecast, and disclosure claims; route adjacent authority to the correct owner.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Client master record, contract, SOW, service catalog, SLA table, client scorecard requirement, and authorization matrix
Ticket, case, work-order, queue, service desk, workflow, and escalation records
Service evidence binder, intake packet, approval packet, document repository index, e-signature record, and correspondence archive
Metric dictionary, denominator rules, exclusion rules, source-precedence policy, target thresholds, cadence, audience, and publication approval chain
BI extract, dashboard definition, report query, refresh timestamp, data-quality profile, and methodology-change log
Audit logs, access roster, IAM extract, retention schedule, legal-hold flag, redaction log, and confidentiality classification
Exception register, reopened-work log, SLA breach log, backlog aging report, reconciliation note, and data-quality escalation record
systems_of_record: Client relationship management or client master system
Contract lifecycle management and SOW repository
Service desk, ticketing, workflow, case management, or work-order system
Document management or electronic records management system
E-signature, approval workflow, and correspondence archive
Business intelligence, reporting, or data warehouse layer
Policy, service catalog, and metric dictionary repository
Audit log, GRC, control management, or evidence repository
Retention, legal-hold, privacy, and records-governance system

allowed: Classify service administration analytics requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Define administrative service metrics, source hierarchy, denominator logic, refresh assumptions, caveats, and action thresholds.
Prepare evidence-backed analytical briefs, report packages, reconciliation notes, data-quality escalations, disclosure-risk notes, and tenant-adaptation notes.
escalate: Tenant service model, jurisdiction, client contract, service catalog, metric dictionary, source hierarchy, record location, access owner, retrieval hook, publication audience, or approval chain is missing.
Public baseline, tenant policy, client SOW, service catalog, retention/access controls, and operational practice conflict in ways that materially change the metric or publication posture.
Operations, compliance, legal, privacy, records governance, finance, HR, security, safety, or executive approval authority is required.
refuse: Request asks for legal advice, formal certification, regulator negotiation, privacy interpretation, record disposition, staffing approval, client credit, contract change, or final client-facing signoff.
Request asks the lane to publish unsupported causal, benchmark, forecast, policy, disclosure, or certainty claims.
Request would report metrics without required evidence, current source-system confirmation, metric definitions, source lineage, confidentiality checks, or owner traceability.

domain_failure_modes: Treating the industry name as cosmetic and producing generic productivity analytics that ignore NAICS 561 support-service scope, client/SOW boundaries, service queues, evidence binders, and confidentiality controls.
Publishing SLA attainment, completion rate, backlog aging, or evidence-readiness metrics when denominator rules, source hierarchy, refresh windows, or approval context are missing or disputed.
Resolving conflicts between service desk, workflow queue, document repository, audit log, BI, client scorecard, tenant policy, and SOW records without an authorized source-of-truth owner.
Using stale or incomplete records to support client-facing scorecards, trend claims, benchmarks, forecasts, or audit-readiness statements.

Confusing descriptive administrative service analytics with approval authority over staffing, pricing, client credits, SLA exceptions, contract amendments, records disposition, privacy decisions, or safety/security incident handling.
Combining cross-client or restricted administrative service evidence without tenant-approved confidentiality, access, retention, legal-hold, or redaction context.

Using one generic template across materially different NAICS 561 service families such as office administration, call centers, document processing, business process outsourcing, collections, facilities support, and security support.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports
Normal in-scope execution with realistic terminology
Low-confidence due missing facts or authority sources

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Which administrative service family controls the metric set
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: NAICS 561 contains materially different service families; runtime must retrieve service family, SOW scope, tenant service catalog, jurisdiction, client contract, and adjacent-lane context before case-specific output.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific metric definitions and source precedence
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public sources constrain definition and disclosure discipline, but denominators, exclusions, refresh windows, owner maps, and source hierarchy must be supplied by the tenant. Missing values become blocked-prerequisite or escalation behavior.
The pack supports disclosure-risk identification and safe caveats only. Final client, regulator, investor, legal, or public release signoff remains outside the lane.
